title: Pre-season attendance at 74% — base fitness at risk
description: Senior A turnout at 74% in week 1 of pre-season. Early absence patterns tend to persist through the season.
explanation: Pre-season turnout below 85% triggers this because base fitness built now directly influences competitive-phase performance.
action: Communicate the importance of pre-season commitment to all players.
notes: Sent WhatsApp blast — attendance improved to 86% by week 3.

title: Under 18s — 4 youth players above recommended weekly session limit
description: Liam Carroll, Darragh Walsh, Rory Quinn, and Cian Murphy logged 4+ sessions in week 1. Youth athletes require mandatory rest days.
explanation: Youth players (U18 and below) are flagged when session count exceeds 3 per week due to growth-plate injury risk.
action: Remove these players from Thursday's additional conditioning session. Notify parents.
notes: Session modified. Parents notified via app.

title: 3 active injuries entering pre-season competitive block
description: Jack O'Sullivan (hamstring), Conor Lynch (knee), and Ross Dunne (shoulder) all carrying injuries into the first contact block.
explanation: When 3+ active injuries are recorded simultaneously, a medical review is raised to check for systemic causes (overtraining, pitch conditions, warm-up protocols).
action: Review training load data for the previous 3 weeks. Flag cluster to medical officer.
notes: Medical officer reviewed — no systemic cause identified. Isolated incidents.

title: Pre-season phase: recommended focus — Aerobic base, structural fitness
description: You are in the pre-season phase. Evidence-based prescription: aerobic base (GPS volume), structural conditioning, and system introduction.
explanation: Season intelligence maps the current date to the pre-season phase and generates a training prescription based on rugby periodisation models.
action: Align next 4 sessions with aerobic base emphasis. Cap contact sessions at 1 per week.

title: 2 Prop players unavailable — 5d to first pre-season fixture
description: Jack O'Sullivan and Conor Lynch are both unavailable for the pre-season opener vs Naas RFC.
explanation: Two players unavailable in the same position creates a structural gap. Selection action needed when positional group drops below minimum cover.
action: Review squad depth at Prop. Consider repositioning or calling up cover.
notes: Moved Cormac to loosehead. Called up Fionn from Senior B.

title: Member engagement score 54/100 — communication review needed
description: Engagement across digital touchpoints has dropped to 54/100. Members are less active in the app and email open rates are down.
explanation: Engagement is the leading indicator for renewal and retention, typically falling 4–6 weeks before membership lapses accelerate.
action: Schedule a newsletter and launch a pre-season community event.
notes: Pre-season newsletter sent. Open rate 61% — above average.

title: Ross Dunne — concussion protocol active, not cleared for contact
description: Ross Dunne received a head impact in Friday's training session. Concussion assessment completed — currently in Return-to-Play step 2.
explanation: Any HIGH severity medical alert triggers this recommendation because contact clearance is a statutory safeguarding requirement.
action: Contact medical officer. Update Ross's status before next training session. Do not permit contact until step 5 clearance.
notes: RTP protocol followed. Ross cleared for contact 7 days later. Missed Bective fixture.

title: Competitive season: recommended focus — Game shape, Set-piece refinement
description: You are in the competitive season phase. Prescription: reduce volume, increase specificity. Focus on game patterns and set-piece.
explanation: Season intelligence tracks your club's position and maps it to competitive-phase prescriptions. Volume should decrease as match frequency increases.
action: Run one pattern session and one defensive session before each matchday.

title: Heavy rain forecast — reduce kicking volume
description: Heavy rain forecast for Thursday and Friday training windows ahead of Saturday vs Bective Rangers.
explanation: Weather risk above CLEAR triggers logistics recommendations. Heavy rain makes kicking practice less effective and can reinforce poor technique.
action: Move kicking sessions to the sports hall. Prioritise contact and defensive shape work.
notes: Hall not available. Went ahead outdoors — no issues.

title: Match week: reduce training load to protect freshness
description: Fixture vs Bective Rangers is 3 days away. This is the optimal window to taper training intensity.
explanation: Season intelligence detects match-week prep (3d to kickoff). Load reduction is recommended automatically in the final 3 days before any fixture.
action: Cap session length to 60 min. Focus on shape and set-pieces. No contact above 50%.

title: 2 losses in last 3 matches — tactical review recommended
description: Last 3 results: L 7-22, L 14-19, W 18-12. Two consecutive defeats before the win suggest a systematic issue.
explanation: When 2+ losses appear in a 3-match window, the pattern is flagged as potentially systematic rather than one-off.
action: Schedule a coaches' video review. Identify one recurring theme and address it in the next two training sessions.
notes: Video review: defensive line speed. Addressed in 2 sessions. Won next match 23-10.

title: Senior B attendance has dropped 22% this month
description: Senior B average attendance is 61% this month, down from 83% last month.
explanation: Attendance engine is tracking a 22% decline in Senior B training turnout. Fires when attendance falls below 80% or shows consistent downward trend.
action: Run an attendance communication. Investigate scheduling conflicts with the coaching group.
notes: Coaching group believes Tuesday session clashes with GAA. Considering time change.

title: Séan Hennessy flagged at welfare risk — attendance 48%
description: Séan Hennessy's attendance has dropped to 48% over the last 6 weeks. The digital twin has assigned a high retention-risk score.
explanation: The digital twin assigns welfare risk scores based on attendance, injury history, and engagement signals. Players crossing the high-risk threshold trigger this.
action: Make direct contact with Séan this week. Log any welfare notes in the player profile.
notes: Spoke with Séan — work pressures. Will return after project deadline.

title: 2 Lock players unavailable — 6d to Terenure fixture
description: Fionn Brennan (work commitment) and Ciarán Kelly (hamstring) are unavailable for the Terenure match.
explanation: Two players unavailable in the same position 6 days out triggers HIGH-priority selection action.
action: Review lineout options. Consider promoting Under 20s cover player.
notes: Promoted Aaron McCarthy (U20s). Lineout functioned well.

title: Back-to-back fixtures 6d apart — manage squad load
description: Terenure (Sat) and Clontarf (following Fri) are only 6 days apart. Players in both squads need active load management.
explanation: Fixture engine detects two upcoming fixtures within a 7-day window. Load management is triggered for gaps of 7 days or less.
action: Create A/B squad rotation. Limit full-contact training between fixtures. Prioritise recovery.
notes: Rotated 6 players for Clontarf. Managed well — no fatigue injuries.

title: Conor Lynch returning from injury near match day
description: Conor Lynch is due to return from his knee injury 4 days before the Clontarf fixture. Returning players carry elevated re-injury risk in the first 2 sessions back.
explanation: Injury register shows a player with a return date that coincides with the fixture window. Re-injury risk is highest in the first week back.
action: Apply graduated return-to-play protocol. No full contact until medical clearance.
notes: Conor cleared for contact 48h before match. Started as sub.

title: Club health score 56/100 — below healthy threshold
description: The Club Intelligence score has fallen to 56/100. The weakest dimension is engagement at 49/100.
explanation: The Club Intelligence engine continuously models club health across multiple dimensions. A score below 65/100 triggers an early-warning recommendation.
action: Review the Club Intelligence dashboard. Focus improvement efforts on the engagement dimension.
notes: Committee meeting scheduled to review member engagement strategy.

title: Match week: reduce training load to protect freshness
description: Fixture vs Clontarf RFC is 4 days away. Optimal taper window.
explanation: Season intelligence detects match-week prep (4d to kickoff). Load reduction recommended in the final 3 days before any fixture.
action: Cap session at 60 min. Set-pieces and shape only. No contact above 50%.

title: Three consecutive wins — protect mental freshness
description: Wins vs Terenure (23-10), Clontarf (17-14), and Naas (21-7). A winning run can create complacency in training.
explanation: Three consecutive wins trigger a low-priority reminder to help coaches avoid complacency and maintain process focus.
action: Run a session focused on areas of improvement. Set new micro-targets rather than celebrating past wins.

title: 2 Prop players unavailable — 5d to kickoff
description: Jack O'Sullivan (hamstring) and Conor Lynch (knee, precautionary) are listed as unavailable for Saturday's fixture.
explanation: Fixture squad status shows 2 players unavailable in the same position (Prop) within match week. Positional shortage below minimum cover requires immediate selection action.
action: Review squad depth at Prop. Consider repositioning or calling up cover from the development squad.

title: 1 high-severity medical alert — immediate review required
description: Ross Dunne has an active concussion protocol flag. Not cleared for contact.
explanation: Any HIGH severity medical alert triggers this because contact clearance is a safeguarding requirement.
action: Contact the club's medical officer today. Update Ross's status before the next training session.

title: Training attendance has dropped 18% this month
description: Average club-wide attendance is 71%. Senior A lowest at 63%.
explanation: Attendance engine tracking a 18% decline in average training turnout. Fires when attendance falls below 80% or shows a consistent downward trend.
action: Run attendance communication to all teams. Investigate scheduling conflicts.

title: 1 player flagged at welfare risk
description: Séan Hennessy has been flagged again. Attendance has not recovered since the last check-in.
explanation: Digital twin welfare risk scoring re-flagged Séan after attendance remained below 50% for 3 consecutive weeks.
action: Make direct contact with Séan this week. Consider a pastoral conversation, not just a training reminder.

title: Club health score 58/100 — below healthy threshold
description: Engagement 52/100, attendance 68/100, governance 71/100, finance 61/100.
explanation: Club Intelligence score below 65/100 triggers this early warning. Engagement remains the weakest dimension.
action: Review the Club Intelligence dashboard. Prioritise engagement improvement.

title: Season phase: Competitive Season — recommended focus: Game shape, Set-piece refinement
description: Mid-competitive phase. Prescription: maintain competitive fitness, sharpen game patterns. Run one pattern session and one defensive session per match week.
explanation: Season intelligence maps current date to competitive phase prescription. Volume should decrease as match frequency holds steady.
action: Align next 2 sessions with set-piece refinement and defensive organisation.

title: Heavy rain forecast — reduce kicking volume
description: Heavy rain forecast for Thursday and Friday training windows.
explanation: Weather risk above CLEAR for the training window. Heavy rain makes kicking practice less effective.
action: Move kicking sessions indoors or postpone. Prioritise contact and defensive shape.
